texto: Stung, I lifted my eyes to his and saw them as if for the first time. Eyes the color of rain, soft as dew and strong enough to etch a mountainside. Tears shimmered there — tears, ay Mother! Or maybe they were in my own eyes.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Deborah Wheeler: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Stung, I lifted my eyes to his and saw them as if for the first time. Eyes the color of rain, sof…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, tears, stung pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e reflexao.

frase_original: Stung, I lifted my eyes to his and saw them as if for the first time. Eyes the color of rain, soft as dew and strong enough to etch a mountainside. Tears shimmered there — tears, ay Mother! Or maybe they were in my own eyes.

texto: He stared at Esmelda with a face like glass, nothing hidden. What I saw there wasn’t steel or fire or stone. Feelings stirred in me and I had to look away. I knew what I saw because I’d felt them, too — understanding, sadness, compassion...forgiveness.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Deborah Wheeler: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “He stared at Esmelda with a face like glass, nothing hidden. What I saw there wasn’t steel or fir…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, feelings, stared pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: It was past dark when I reached the city and I’d mostly shoved my ghosts back into their graves. I let the gray mare pick her own pace and browse in the grain fields along the way.
explicacao: Citação de Deborah Wheeler sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “It was past dark when I reached the city and I’d mostly shoved my ghosts back into their graves. …” articula reflexao, ghosts, reached em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e reflexao-noturna.
